pr_analysis_patterns.overview: Comprehensive patterns and techniques for analyzing pull requests that contain vulnerability fixes, understanding the security context, and identifying potential breaking changes that may cause test failures.

item.name: package_changes
item.description: Identify which packages are being upgraded

what_to_extract.item: Whether it's a direct or transitive dependency

item.name: version_jump_analysis
item.description: Assess the magnitude of the version change

major_version.action: Expect significant API changes, review migration guide

minor_version.action: Check for deprecations and new features

patch_version.action: Review bug fixes, minimal changes expected

item.name: test_failure_indicators
item.description: Identify signs of test failures in PR

direct_dependency.definition: Package explicitly listed in your dependency file that you directly import/use
direct_dependency.identification: - Listed in requirements.txt, package.json, etc. - Your code has import statements for this package - You call functions/classes from this package
direct_dependency.remediation_approach: - Update the version in dependency file - Fix any breaking changes in your code - Update imports if package structure changed

transitive_dependency.definition: Package required by one of your direct dependencies, not used directly by your code
transitive_dependency.identification: - Not listed in your main dependency file - Appears in lock files (package-lock.json, Pipfile.lock) - Your code doesn't import it directly
transitive_dependency.remediation_approach: - Upgrade the direct dependency that requires it - Use dependency overrides/resolutions if needed - Verify compatibility with direct dependency

dependency_tree_analysis.purpose: Understand the full impact of a package upgrade on the dependency tree

what_to_look_for.item: Which packages depend on the upgraded package
Other packages that might need upgrading

source.name: Official Migration Guide
source.description: Most packages provide migration guides for major version upgrades

locations.location: Release notes for the new version

source.name: CHANGELOG
source.description: Detailed list of changes between versions

source.name: Release Notes
source.description: High-level overview of changes in each release

source.name: API Documentation
source.description: Compare old vs new API documentation
source.approach: - Look at docs for old version - Compare with docs for new version - Note any missing or changed APIs

source.name: Community Resources
source.description: Learn from others who have upgraded

resources.resource: Stack Overflow questions about the upgrade
GitHub issues in the package repository

change.description: Function parameters added, removed, or reordered
change.example: Old: function(arg1, arg2) New: function(arg1, arg2, required_arg3)
change.detection: TypeError: function() takes X arguments but Y were given

change.description: Function return type or structure changed
change.example: Old: returns dict New: returns custom object
Old: package.old_function() New: function no longer exists
Old: from package import function New: from package.submodule import function
Old: from package.old_name import Class New: from package.new_name import Class

change.detection: Tests fail due to different behavior with same code

change.example: Old: accepts any string New: validates string format
Old: raises ValueError New: raises CustomException

change.description: Configuration file format or structure changed
change.example: Old: JSON config New: YAML config required
Old: optional setting New: required setting

item.name: dependency_file_changes
item.analysis: - Note exact version changes - Check for multiple package upgrades - Look for comments explaining changes - Identify if it's a minimal or comprehensive upgrade

item.name: code_changes
item.description: Any code changes accompanying the upgrade
item.significance: If DevOps engineer already made code changes, these indicate known breaking changes they've addressed. Your job is to fix the remaining issues causing test failures.

item.name: test_changes
item.analysis: - Updated test expectations - New test setup requirements - Changed mock configurations - These show what the engineer knows needs updating

item.name: documentation_updates
item.value: May contain notes about the upgrade, known issues, or migration steps

step.details: Understand what packages changed and by how much
See what the DevOps engineer already fixed
Understand what test updates were attempted

step.action: Look for comments in the diff
step.details: Engineers often leave helpful comments explaining changes

cve_research.purpose: Understand what vulnerability is being fixed to ensure your remediation doesn't compromise the security fix

questions.question: Which functions or features are vulnerable?
What conditions trigger the vulnerability?
How does the upgrade fix it?

step.verification: - Confirm the new version addresses the CVE - Check if there are any workarounds mentioned - Ensure no code changes reintroduce the vulnerability

constraint.name: no_version_downgrade
constraint.rule: Never downgrade to a vulnerable version
constraint.rationale: Even if tests pass with an older version, security takes priority

constraint.name: no_workarounds_that_expose
constraint.rule: Don't implement workarounds that bypass security fixes
constraint.example: Bad: Disabling input validation to make tests pass Good: Updating test inputs to match new validation

constraint.name: maintain_security_features
constraint.rule: Keep any new security features enabled
constraint.example: If upgrade adds CSRF protection, don't disable it to fix tests

section.name: basic_information
section.item: ✓ PR author and creation date recorded

analysis_output_template.title: PR Analysis Summary Template
